name: Riverside.fm Recording API
what_it_does: Professional remote recording platform with REST API — enabling high-quality local audio and video recording for podcasts and video shows directly in the browser, with separate track recording, AI transcription, automatic post-production, and media export for editing.
use_cases: Automating Riverside recording session creation for scheduled podcast episodes
Retrieving recording files and transcripts after completed sessions via API
Integrating Riverside recordings with podcast CMS or video editing workflow
Building custom recording dashboards for podcast production teams
Syncing episode recordings to storage and triggering post-production pipelines
not_for: Live streaming and broadcast (use Restream or StreamYard for live output)
Video conferencing and team meetings (use Zoom or Teams)
best_when: A podcast or video show creator needs studio-quality remote recordings with separate audio/video tracks per participant, AI transcription, and automated post-production in one platform.
avoid_when: You need live streaming, webinar features, or real-time collaboration editing rather than asynchronous recording and production.

auth.notes: API key from Riverside account settings. Bearer token auth. Workspace-scoped access. Webhook for recording completion events. No OAuth2. Business or above plan required for API access.

pricing.notes: Israeli company (Tel Aviv). Founded 2019. Raised $35M+. Sequoia-backed. Local recording technology ensures quality regardless of internet instability. AI transcription and magic clips features. Used by major podcast networks and media companies. Strong creator economy adoption.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for studios, sessions, recordings, and transcripts. Bearer token auth. Webhook for session completion. Good API documentation for core workflows. Recordings are large files — use pre-signed URLs for download. Transcript processing is async after recording completes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Session reads fully idempotent. Recording reads idempotent. Transcript reads idempotent once processed. Session creation idempotent by name within workspace. File download URLs are time-limited.
known_agent_gotchas: API access requires Business plan or above — not available on free/Standard
Recordings are large files — use download URLs rather than inline API transfer
Transcript processing is asynchronous — poll webhook or status endpoint
Download URLs for recordings are time-limited (pre-signed) — don't cache long-term
Separate audio tracks per participant increase storage and processing time

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US and EU data residency. Bearer token with workspace scope. Recording files may contain sensitive interview content — secure download URL handling. SOC2 certification status unclear — verify for enterprise use.
